sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Withdraw treasury funds for Phase 2 of a Cardano-native ticketing platform operated by Sellout.io and built by Anvil Development Agency.

Deliverables: secondary marketplace with on-chain settlement, smart-contract royalty enforcement, per-event anti-scalping controls, wallet onboarding for Sellout users, organizer tools, independent third-party security audit, public launch campaign and trade-show activations, public metrics dashboard and quarterly reporting

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Phase 1 of the ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet with on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Sellout funded Phase 1 entirely from its own capital with over $130,000 invested.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, stated as approximately $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, including a 3% Intersect administration fee.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Phase 2 is planned to deliver a secondary marketplace, royalty enforcement, anti-scalping controls, wallet onboarding, organizer tools, an independent audit, and launch marketing.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The team commits to repay $1,093,231 to the Cardano Treasury through a revenue-share mechanism, with 25% of fees paid until reimbursement and 5% thereafter.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Sellout is described as having 200,000+ registered users, 250,000+ tickets issued to date, and 350+ events annually at current cadence.
missing: Independent evidence for: Phase 1 of the ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet with on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification.
Independent evidence for: Sellout funded Phase 1 entirely from its own capital with over $130,000 invested.
Independent evidence for: Sellout is described as having 200,000+ registered users, 250,000+ tickets issued to date, and 350+ events annually at current cadence.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states that Anvil and Sellout have not received ada from the Cardano Treasury within the last 24 months.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, stated as approximately $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, including a 3% Intersect administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Phase 1 of the ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet with on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
